item.title: fleet.json written without mode 0600 — agent registry world-readable on multi-user systems
item.intent: fleet.json must be written with mode 0600 — agent registry (names, paths, health) must not be readable by other users on the system.
item.description: src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts line 67 calls writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) without a mode option when writing the fleet registry atomically. The cloud/ parent directory is correctly created with DIR_MODE_SECRET (line 59), but the fleet.json file itself is written with default permissions (0644). fleet.json contains: registered agent names, deployment directories (absolute paths), and health metadata — exposing the full inventory of agents and their filesystem locations to any user on the system. Fix: add FILE_MODE_SECRET to the import from config/defaults.js; add chmodSync to the node:fs import; change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }); add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) after the write.
steps: Read src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts lines 10-14 — confirm chmodSync is not in node:fs import and FILE_MODE_SECRET is not in the defaults import (currently only DIR_MODE_SECRET)
Line 10: add chmodSync to the node:fs import destructure — result: import { chmodSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, renameSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
Line 13: add FILE_MODE_SECRET to the defaults import — change to: import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from '../../config/defaults.js';
Line 67 (inside writeFleetRegistry try block): change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET });
After line 67: add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET); — this runs before renameSync so the temp file has correct mode before atomic rename
Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern='fleet|discovery' and confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: fleet.json is written with mode 0600 — stat on ~/.clawhq/cloud/fleet.json confirms no group/other read bits
The cloud/ parent directory remains at mode 0700 (unchanged — was already correct)
All existing fleet/discovery tests pass with no regressions
